extracted_information: StackBlitz WebContainers enable running fullstack Node.js environments natively within the browser, leveraging WebAssembly and new browser capabilities APIs. This provides a development environment that is faster, more secure, and consistent than traditional local or remote server-based setups. It eliminates the need for local installations of Node.js, npm, and git, allowing for instant project booting, seamless collaboration, and enhanced security by operating entirely within the browser's security sandbox.

specifications.node_js_execution: Natively in-browser (Node.js runtime runs inside the browser, not on remote servers)

performance_metrics.build_speed_improvement: Up to 20% faster than local environments
performance_metrics.latency: Less latency than localhost for server responses

specifications.security_model: Browser's security sandbox (all code execution occurs inside), protects from localhost scraping attacks, addresses long-standing npm vulnerability
specifications.network_stack: Virtualized TCP network stack mapped to browser's ServiceWorker API
specifications.environment_management: Fresh environment on every page load, built-in npm client
specifications.api_availability: WebContainers API available, self-hosting possible with StackBlitz Enterprise

features: Native back-end debugging with Chrome DevTools (no installs/extensions needed)
Ability to run live Node.js servers in-browser
Zero footprint (no server resources, no `node_modules` on local machine)
Integrated with Chrome PWA functionality for desktop app install
Future potential for local filesystem access via File System Access API
Supports Next.js, GraphQL, Vanilla Node.js (as of public beta launch)
